records.title: Supreme Court narrows Section 2 redistricting protections in Louisiana v. Callais
records.promise_text: In Louisiana v. Callais, the Supreme Court held that Louisiana's remedial second majority-Black congressional district was an unconstitutional racial gerrymander, concluded that Section 2 did not require an additional majority-minority district on the record before it, and revised the Gingles framework to require stronger proof disentangling race from politics and supporting an inference of intentional discrimination.
records.impacted_group: Black voters and other minority voters whose ability to challenge vote dilution under Section 2 depends on federal redistricting enforcement
records.summary: The Court invalidated Louisiana's remedial second majority-Black district and made Section 2 vote-dilution claims harder to win without formally repealing the statute.
records.notes: Modeled as a current-term judicial action because the ruling changed the live voting-rights enforcement environment during the current administration. This record does not treat the White House as the author of the opinion; it tracks the judicial action's public impact during the term.

promise_sources.source_title: Louisiana v. Callais, 608 U.S. ___ (2026) slip opinion
promise_sources.publisher: Supreme Court of the United States

actions.title: Supreme Court decides Louisiana v. Callais
actions.description: The Court affirmed the invalidation of Louisiana's SB8 map, held that Section 2 did not require an additional majority-minority district on this record, and revised the Gingles framework to require stronger race-versus-politics and intentional-discrimination showings.

action_sources.source_title: Louisiana v. Callais, 608 U.S. ___ (2026) slip opinion
action_sources.publisher: Supreme Court of the United States

outcomes.outcome_summary: The ruling invalidated Louisiana's remedial second majority-Black congressional district and narrowed the proof path for Section 2 redistricting claims without formally striking Section 2 from the statute books.
outcomes.measurable_impact: Voters challenging vote dilution under Section 2 now face a higher burden to show maps denied equal electoral opportunity, including tighter race-versus-politics proof requirements and an intentional-discrimination inference standard.
outcomes.black_community_impact_note: The clearest Black-impact channel is political power. Black voters in Louisiana and other jurisdictions relying on Section 2 redistricting suits face weaker federal protection against vote dilution and a harder path to securing effective opportunity districts.

outcome_sources.source_title: Louisiana v. Callais, 608 U.S. ___ (2026) slip opinion
outcome_sources.publisher: Supreme Court of the United States

outcome_sources.source_title: Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act at the Supreme Court
outcome_sources.notes: Doctrine and litigation context for the Section 2 standards at issue.

outcome_sources.source_title: NAACP Condemns Supreme Court's Ruling Against Voting Rights
